question_text: A vessel crossing a Traffic Separation Scheme should normally cross the traffic lanes:
option_b: At right angles to the general direction of traffic flow

question_text: If successive fixes show the vessel being set to starboard, the OOW should:
option_b: Alter as required and monitor with fresh fixes
option_c: Ignore the set if speed is steady
option_d: Stop engines until the tide changes

question_text: For a visual fix, the best charted objects are those that are:
option_a: Conspicuous, accurately charted, and well separated
option_b: Closest to the ship regardless of charting

question_text: The chart notation Fl(3) 10s indicates:
option_a: A fixed light shown for 10 seconds
option_b: Three flashes every 10 seconds

question_text: A vessel has 180 miles to run at 15 knots. The passage time is:
option_b: 11 hours

question_text: When planning landfall, the most critical consideration is:
option_b: Expected set, drift, visibility, and reliable fixing marks

question_text: To compare an echo sounder reading with charted depth, the navigator must allow for:
option_b: Tidal height and the transducer datum used

question_text: At anchor, the earliest reliable sign of dragging is usually:
option_b: Ship's position moving relative to fixed references

question_text: TPC is the weight required to:
option_b: Change mean draught by 1 cm

question_text: Fresh Water Allowance is the change in draught when moving:
option_a: From fresh water to dock water
option_b: From salt water to fresh water

question_text: If TPC is 18 tonnes, the weight needed to increase mean draught by 20 cm is:
option_b: 240 tonnes

question_text: Dock Water Allowance is used to correct for:
option_b: Water density differing from standard sea water

question_text: Righting moment at a given heel angle equals:
option_b: KM - KG

question_text: A stiff ship is likely to have:
option_a: A quick short roll and relatively large GM
option_b: A long slow roll and small GM

question_text: Which statement is correct?
option_a: List is due to off-centre weight; loll is linked with very small or negative GM
option_b: List and loll mean the same
option_c: Loll is caused only by wind
option_d: List occurs only in dry dock

question_text: Free surface in a slack tank will normally:
option_b: Reduce effective GM

question_text: If a weight is shifted to starboard, the vessel will usually:
option_b: List to starboard

question_text: The angle of vanishing stability is the angle at which:
option_b: Maximum GZ occurs

question_text: A trimming moment is found from:
option_b: Displacement x GM

question_text: Loading a weight well forward of the LCF will tend to:
option_a: Trim the ship by the head
option_b: Trim the ship by the stern

question_text: MCTC is the moment required to:
option_b: Change mean draught by 1 cm

question_text: If draughts are F 7.8 m and A 8.4 m, the mean draught is:
A ship on even keel has:
option_b: Equal draught forward and aft

question_text: Lifting a heavy load by ship's gear will usually:
option_b: Raise the effective KG and reduce GM
option_d: Have no effect until landed ashore

question_text: For radar fixing, the best targets are generally:
option_b: Prominent charted objects with clear definition

question_text: Compass error obtained by azimuth is the angle between:
option_b: Ship's head and wake
option_c: Magnetic north and grid north only

question_text: In restricted visibility, safe navigation is best supported by:
option_b: Proper lookout, systematic radar use, and safe speed
option_c: Maintaining full sea speed if on schedule

question_text: The publication used to promulgate chart corrections is:
option_b: Notices to Mariners

question_text: The rule of twelfths provides:
option_b: A simple approximation for semidiurnal tidal height change

question_text: Parallel indexing is mainly used to:
option_b: Monitor track relative to a planned line or hazard

question_text: An overtaking vessel must keep clear until:
option_b: The stand-on vessel alters course
option_c: She is finally past and clear

question_text: Gyro error at high latitude is often affected by:
option_b: Tide height

question_text: A passage plan should be amended when:
option_b: New information or conditions make revision necessary

question_text: Before coastal pilotage, the bridge team should focus on:
option_b: Master-pilot exchange, expected tracks, and no-go areas

question_text: To reduce free surface effect, the best general action is to:
option_b: Press up or empty slack tanks where possible

question_text: A vessel required to proceed at safe speed must consider:
option_b: Visibility, traffic density, manoeuvrability, and background light

question_text: A danger bearing is primarily used to:
option_b: Warn that a vessel is standing into danger if a bearing is reached
option_c: Check compass deviation on all headings

question_text: When taking a three-point bearing fix, the greatest risk is:
option_b: Time delay causing running-fix error if the ship is moving fast

question_text: A clearing bearing is intended to:
option_a: Keep the vessel clear of a charted danger
option_b: Measure gyro repeaters

question_text: When converting relative radar motion to true motion mentally, the OOW must remember that relative vectors show:
option_a: Target motion over the ground only
option_b: Motion of targets relative to own ship's motion

question_text: A decreasing barometer with strengthening wind most commonly suggests:
option_b: An approaching low-pressure system

question_text: The main purpose of a no-go area in passage planning is to:
option_b: Define water or positions the vessel must not enter

question_text: The standard sound signal for a power-driven vessel making way in fog is:
option_b: Two short blasts every minute

question_text: A vessel not under command shall exhibit by day:
option_b: Two black balls in a vertical line
option_c: Three black shapes in a line

question_text: When using ECDIS route monitoring, a key reason to retain paperless vigilance is that:
option_a: ECDIS removes the need for lookout
option_b: Alarm settings and displayed layers may not by themselves ensure safe navigation
option_c: Courses cannot be altered on ECDIS

question_text: If the echo sounder suddenly reads much less water than expected in a narrow channel, the OOW should first:
option_a: Assume instrument failure and ignore it
option_b: Reduce risk by checking position, speed, and under-keel situation immediately

question_text: A wheel-over position is planned to:
option_a: Show where the anchor should be let go
option_b: Indicate where the turn should be started to achieve the next track

question_text: The principal benefit of a bridge team briefing before departure is to:
option_b: Create shared understanding of the plan, hazards, and responsibilities
option_d: Allow the OOW to leave early

question_text: If the observed compass bearing is east of the true bearing, the compass error is:
option_b: Westerly

question_text: A vessel in the vicinity of a narrow channel should:
option_a: Anchor in the channel if convenient
option_b: Keep as near to the outer limit on her starboard side as is safe and practicable
option_c: Use any side with less traffic

question_text: The best estimate of tidal stream set and drift during a coastal passage is obtained from:
option_b: Tidal stream atlas or current predictions combined with observed fixes

question_text: Leeway is the movement of a vessel caused mainly by:
option_b: Wind acting on the vessel

question_text: A running fix is most useful when:
option_a: Only one object can be observed at different times
option_b: Three simultaneous bearings are available
option_c: Radar is unavailable in all respects

question_text: When taking over a navigational watch, the relieving officer should first satisfy himself about:
option_b: The ship's position, course, speed, and prevailing circumstances

question_text: If CPA on ARPA is reducing to an unsafe value, the OOW should:
option_a: Wait until visual range is zero
option_b: Take early and substantial action in accordance with COLREGS
option_d: Ignore ARPA until the master arrives

question_text: Which factor increases squat?
option_b: Shallow water and higher speed

question_text: In heavy weather, a key reason for securing watertight openings is to:
option_b: Reduce risk of downflooding and loss of reserve buoyancy

question_text: The purpose of an under-keel clearance policy is to:
option_b: Ensure a defined minimum margin beneath the keel for safety

question_text: A broad reach under sail for a sailing vessel means the wind is:
option_b: Abaft the beam

question_text: For cargo securing, the primary objective is to:
option_b: Prevent cargo movement under expected accelerations

question_text: If the vessel is unexpectedly early at a tidal gate, the preferred action is usually to:
option_b: Adjust speed safely or wait in a suitable area according to the plan

question_text: A cardinal mark with two cones pointing up indicates:
option_b: East cardinal

question_text: The range of a light shown on a chart is principally:
option_a: The nominal range under defined meteorological visibility
option_b: The guaranteed radar range
option_c: The distance in cables at spring tide
option_d: The arc of visibility in degrees

question_text: A magnetic compass deviation card records:
option_b: Compass deviation on different headings

question_text: When plotting on Mercator charts, a rhumb line is:
option_a: The shortest distance between two points
option_b: A line cutting all meridians at the same angle
option_c: A great circle on the equator only

question_text: The purpose of a secondary port tidal difference is to:
option_b: Adjust standard port times and heights to estimate local tide

question_text: A vessel constrained by her draught is one which:
option_a: Cannot deviate because of traffic only
option_b: Because of draught in relation to available depth and width is severely restricted in departing from her course
option_d: Is at anchor in deep water

question_text: In a turn, advance is the distance measured:
option_b: Along the original course from wheel-over to the point abeam of the new course

question_text: A Williamson turn is commonly used to:
option_b: Recover a person overboard when immediate return to the reciprocal track is needed

question_text: A vessel exhibiting red over white lights at night is:
option_a: Engaged in fishing other than trawling
option_b: Not under command

question_text: When checking gyro and magnetic headings together, the purpose is mainly to:
option_b: Monitor compass performance and heading reference consistency

question_text: The best immediate action after a man overboard alarm is to:
option_b: Mark position, post lookouts, commence recovery manoeuvre, and inform the master

question_text: If charted depth is 8.0 m, height of tide is 2.2 m, and squat is estimated at 0.4 m, available water depth is:
A vessel's ETA should normally be updated using:
option_b: Current observed progress and revised passage information

question_text: Which action best reduces the risk of fatigue-related errors on watch?
option_b: Following rest hour requirements and maintaining effective watch routines
option_c: Reducing bridge lighting to minimum always

question_text: The bow thruster is most effective when:
option_b: The vessel has little or no headway
option_c: The ship is in deep ocean passage

question_text: When a vessel takes a heavy list due to cargo shift, the first concern is:
option_b: Overall stability and risk of further shift

question_text: In a bridge equipment failure, the OOW should first:
option_b: Assess the impact on navigation, apply contingencies, and inform the master as required

question_text: A transit is particularly useful for:
option_b: Checking that the vessel remains on a defined line

question_text: Which is the best definition of variation?
option_a: Error of the gyro due to latitude
option_b: Angle between true north and magnetic north
option_c: Angle between compass north and ship's head

question_text: If a ship's stern swings to port when going ahead from rest with a right-handed propeller, this is commonly due to:
option_b: Transverse thrust effect

question_text: When is a vessel aground considered stranded for stability purposes?
option_b: When supported by the ground so that normal buoyancy is reduced
option_c: Only when fully high and dry

question_text: A radar blind sector is dangerous because it:
option_b: Prevents target detection in part of the horizon

question_text: The purpose of a course alteration being substantial under COLREGS is to:
option_b: Be readily apparent to another vessel
option_d: Avoid all use of sound signals

question_text: On ECDIS, a safety contour is selected mainly to:
option_b: Highlight depths critical to safe navigation
option_c: Replace the need for tidal calculations

question_text: A vessel's reserve buoyancy is the volume of:
option_b: The watertight hull above the waterline that can contribute to flotation

question_text: If heavy weather is expected on the beam, a prudent preparation is to:
option_b: Check lashings, secure loose gear, and review speed/course options

question_text: The term under way means that a vessel is:
option_a: Making way through the water only
option_b: Not at anchor, made fast, or aground

question_text: A fix obtained from GNSS should still be cross-checked because:
option_a: Satellite navigation is never used at sea
option_b: Single-source errors can occur and bridge practice requires independent monitoring

question_text: A cargo plan should clearly show:
option_b: Distribution of cargo, special requirements, and loading sequence as relevant

question_text: The length of a degree of latitude is approximately:
option_b: 69 nautical miles

question_text: If a vessel is meeting another head-on, each should normally:
option_b: Alter to starboard so as to pass port to port
option_c: Maintain course until collision is certain

question_text: In tidal calculations, chart datum is generally:
option_a: The level of highest astronomical tide
option_b: A reference level to which charted depths are reduced
option_d: The level of spring high water

question_text: Which bridge document records key details of the intended route?
option_b: Passage plan

question_text: The primary effect of shifting a weight vertically upward is to:
option_b: Raise KG and reduce GM

question_text: A vessel approaching a pilot station should prepare by:
option_b: Rigging the pilot ladder correctly and reviewing the boarding arrangement

question_text: At night, a vessel at anchor less than 50 m shall show:
option_a: A white all-round light or one ball by day
option_b: Red and green sidelights only

question_text: When using leading lights, the vessel is on the recommended line when:
option_a: The rear light is open to one side
option_b: The lights are in line vertically
option_c: Only the lower light is visible

question_text: The main value of echo sounder trend monitoring is that it:
option_b: Gives warning of unexpected shoaling or departure from planned water depths

question_text: If CPA is acceptable but TCPA is very short in dense traffic, the OOW should:
option_b: Continue close monitoring and ensure passing remains safe and understandable
option_d: Assume the target will alter away

question_text: A vessel making sternway through the water is still:
option_b: Under way

question_text: The key purpose of a pre-arrival checklist is to:
option_b: Confirm the ship, bridge team, and equipment are ready for the next phase

question_text: Mean draught is used in hydrostatic calculations because it:
option_b: Eliminates all trim effects automatically
option_c: Is the same as aft draught

question_text: A vessel with negative initial GM is liable to:
option_b: Loll until a position of equilibrium is reached

question_text: The lee side when at anchor is the side:
option_b: Sheltered from wind and sea

question_text: When paying out cable to walk back the anchor, the aim is to:
option_b: Control the anchor descent and reduce shock load

question_text: A spring tide occurs when:
option_a: Sun and moon act at right angles
option_b: Sun and moon are aligned, giving greater range

question_text: The distance from the centre of flotation to the centre of gravity affects:
option_a: Trimming moment due to added or shifted weight
option_b: Variation only

question_text: In cargo watches, one important responsibility is to:
option_b: Monitor moorings, draughts, and cargo operation safety

question_text: The purpose of an abort point in a pilotage plan is to:
option_b: Define where the vessel must stop the approach if conditions are not satisfactory
option_c: Mark the point to test whistle

question_text: A sextant index error should be:
option_b: Applied with correct sign to observations

question_text: If the ship enters colder, denser water at constant displacement, the draught will generally:
option_b: Decrease

question_text: A vessel showing ball-diamond-ball by day is:
option_b: Restricted in her ability to manoeuvre

question_text: The key value of ARPA trial manoeuvre is that it:
option_b: Allows the OOW to assess the likely effect of a proposed alteration

question_text: The main risk of excessive stern trim in shallow water is:
option_b: Reduced under-keel clearance aft and possible poor handling

question_text: When a vessel is moored in a tidal port during cargo work, the OOW should regularly check:
option_a: Mooring tensions and ship's position alongside
option_b: Only galley stores

question_text: The best reason for using wheel-over positions rather than turning exactly at a waypoint is to:
option_b: Allow for the ship's turning characteristics and track control

question_text: If fog is encountered unexpectedly, the OOW should usually begin by:
option_b: Reducing to safe speed, sounding signals as required, and increasing lookout and radar use

question_text: The main purpose of weather routeing information on a passage is to:
option_b: Support safe and efficient planning in expected weather conditions

question_text: A vessel's statical stability curve shows the relationship between:
option_b: Speed and rpm

question_text: When correcting a course from magnetic to true, variation is:
option_b: Applied according to east-west sign convention

question_text: A parallel body increase in draught due to loading is estimated mainly from:
option_b: TPC

question_text: A target on the starboard bow with constant bearing and decreasing range indicates:
option_b: Risk of collision may exist

question_text: The purpose of a turn circle test is to determine:
option_b: The vessel's manoeuvring characteristics

question_text: If a compartment floods low down on one side, the ship will typically:
option_b: List and suffer loss of stability due to free surface if water can move

question_text: A practical cross-check on GNSS position during pilotage is:
option_b: Use visual bearings, radar ranges, or transits where available

question_text: The main purpose of keeping a proper lookout is to:
option_b: Make a full appraisal of the situation and risk of collision, grounding, and other dangers

question_text: If the observed under-keel margin is reducing below plan, the OOW should:
option_b: Take timely action such as reducing speed, revising track, or seeking assistance as appropriate

question_text: The critical period after letting go the anchor is used to:
option_b: Confirm the anchor is holding and the vessel remains in a safe position

question_text: The beam of a ship is its:
option_b: Greatest breadth

question_text: If a ship has a list caused by off-centre weight, the most direct correction is to:
option_b: Shift weight or ballast to restore equilibrium

question_text: A wheelhouse poster showing emergency steering changeover is useful because it:
option_b: Helps the bridge team transfer steering quickly in failure conditions

question_text: When entering a TSS lane, a vessel should do so:
option_a: At as small an angle as practicable in the general direction of traffic flow
option_b: At right angles to the lane centreline
option_c: Only by crossing the separation zone first

question_text: The purpose of contour spacing on a chart is to indicate:
option_b: The slope of the seabed

question_text: A vessel may need to call the master under standing orders when:
option_b: Traffic, visibility, or navigational circumstances cause doubt or concern

question_text: The point about which a ship trims for small changes of trim is the:
option_b: Longitudinal centre of flotation

question_text: A radar conspicuous object is valuable because it:
option_b: Provides a stronger and more identifiable radar return

question_text: The main hazard of a following sea in heavy weather is that it can:
option_b: Increase risk of surfing, broaching, or heavy stern impacts

question_text: A vessel making no way but drifting with current is:
option_b: Under way

question_text: The first correction when using a harbour chart after receiving updates is to ensure:
option_b: All relevant Notices to Mariners and temporary information are applied

question_text: The principal reason for a UKC allowance for squat is that squat:
option_b: Can significantly reduce clearance in shallow or confined water at speed

question_text: The weather side of a ship at anchor is the side:
option_b: Exposed to wind and sea

question_text: A trim by the stern means:
option_b: Forward draught exceeds aft draught
option_d: The vessel is on even keel

question_text: The purpose of a loading computer is primarily to:
option_b: Assist in checking stability, strength, and loading condition compliance

question_text: When two bearings are nearly parallel, the resulting fix will usually be:
option_b: Poorly conditioned and less reliable

question_text: A vessel hearing one prolonged and two short blasts in fog should recognise this may be:
option_b: A vessel not under command or restricted in ability to manoeuvre, or similar prescribed category
option_c: A pilot vessel on pilotage duty only

question_text: The main purpose of tank soundings during a voyage is to monitor:
option_b: Quantities and possible unintended changes such as leakage or transfer error

question_text: An increase in GM generally makes the roll period:
option_b: Shorter

question_text: If the OOW is uncertain whether risk of collision exists, he should assume:
option_b: Risk exists

question_text: The charted abbreviation Wk indicates:
option_b: Wreck
